text: Risk management is a crucial skill in business and everyday life. The first step is identifying risks, which means thinking about what could go wrong in a project or situation. Once you spot possible problems, the next stage is to develop mitigation strategies. These are actions you take to reduce the likelihood of a risk occurring or to minimise its impact. However, even with careful planning, some risks may still happen. That’s why you need contingency plans. These are backup options you can use if things go badly. Finally, you cannot forget monitoring. Regularly checking your plan helps you spot new risks early and adjust your strategies when necessary.
